title: Executive Order 7344—Revocation of Executive Order No. 5791 of February 2, 1932, Withdrawing Public Lands in Colorado

flags.justification: A single one-time revocation of a prior land withdrawal under existing statutory authority; no ongoing decision-making authority or position is created or exercised.
No implementing actor or ongoing program exists for which responsibility could be unclear — the order takes effect automatically 'upon the date of the official filing of the plat of resurvey.'
Single narrow purpose: revoke one specific prior land withdrawal.
A land-status revocation has no enforcement dimension.
No incentive structure is created by a status-revocation action.
Not a grant of ongoing authority; the order is self-executing upon a defined future event (filing of the resurvey plat), which is itself a completion trigger, not an authority requiring review.
No existing oversight mechanism is touched.
No regulated party or compliance scheme exists.
Restores the specified township to its prior public-domain status pending a routine resurvey; no broader effects identifiable from the text.
No second agency or mandate is implicated.
No exemptions, waivers, or carve-outs are created.

notes: Validation batch, drawn negative (random sample, seed 20260703, pre-registration v1.1). Near-boilerplate land-withdrawal revocation — floor-score exemplar. First-pass coding; not yet cross-coded for inter-coder reliability.
